title: Implement reify-kernel-occt OCCT FFI binding
analysis: The reify-kernel-occt crate is an empty stub that must implement the GeometryKernel trait (defined in reify-types/src/geometry.rs) using OpenCASCADE Technology (OCCT) via FFI. The trait has 4 methods: execute() for geometry operations (primitives, booleans, modifications, transforms), query() for measurements (volume, area, centroid, bbox), export() for STEP/STL output, and tessellate() for mesh generation. All values cross the boundary as Value::Scalar with SI units (meters). The mock kernel in reify-test-support shows the pattern: a shape store (HashMap<u64, Shape>) with monotonic ID counter. The 9 ignored tests in boundary4_geometry.rs have empty bodies that must be filled in and un-ignored. The approach uses cxx bridge with a thin C++ wrapper layer to call OCCT APIs. Each C++ function catches Standard_Failure exceptions and returns error strings. The Rust OcctKernel struct manages the shape store and extracts f64 values from Value types before crossing the FFI boundary.

prerequisites.description: Set up cxx bridge build infrastructure: add cxx/cxx-build/cc dependencies to Cargo.toml, create build.rs that compiles C++ wrapper and links OCCT libraries (TKernel, TKMath, TKBRep, TKPrim, TKBO, TKFillet, TKTopAlgo, TKSTEP, TKSTEPBase, TKSTEPAttr, TKXCAF, TKXSBase, TKMesh, TKG3d, TKGeomBase, TKShHealing), create cpp/ directory with occt_wrapper.h/.cpp skeleton and src/ffi.rs cxx bridge module with OcctShape opaque type.

Create OcctKernel struct in src/lib.rs with shape store (HashMap<u64, cxx::UniquePtr<ffi::OcctShape>>), next_id counter, helper fn extract_f64(Value) -> Result<f64, GeometryError>, and stub GeometryKernel trait impl that returns appropriate errors for all methods. Verify crate compiles.

Add reify-kernel-occt as dev-dependency to crates/reify-eval/Cargo.toml. Add 'use reify_kernel_occt::OcctKernel;' import to the occt_tests module in boundary4_geometry.rs so boundary tests can instantiate the real kernel.

steps.description: Write failing test for Box creation + STEP export: create OcctKernel, execute GeometryOp::Box(10mm x 10mm x 10mm), export to STEP, assert output is non-empty and contains 'ISO-10303-21'. Also fill in boundary test create_box_export_step with same logic and remove #[ignore].

Implement C++ make_box(w, h, d) using BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox centered at (-w/2, -h/2, -d/2) and export_step(shape) using STEPControl_Writer writing to string. Wire into ffi.rs bridge. Implement OcctKernel::execute for GeometryOp::Box and OcctKernel::export for ExportFormat::Step.

Write failing test for Volume query on 10mm box: create 10x10x10mm box, query Volume, assert result ≈ 1e-6 m³ (tolerance 1e-9). Fill in boundary test box_volume_10mm with same logic and remove #[ignore].
Implement C++ query_volume(shape) using BRepGProp::VolumeProperties + GProp_GProps.Mass(). Implement OcctKernel::query for GeometryQuery::Volume returning Value::Real(volume_m3).
Write failing test for Cylinder creation + volume query: create cylinder r=5mm h=20mm, query Volume, assert ≈ π·(0.005)²·0.02 m³. Fill in boundary test cylinder_volume_query and remove #[ignore].
Implement C++ make_cylinder(radius, height) using BRepPrimAPI_MakeCylinder. Wire into OcctKernel::execute for GeometryOp::Cylinder.

Write failing test for Boolean difference: create two overlapping boxes, execute Difference, export to STEP, assert valid non-empty output. Fill in boundary test boolean_difference and remove #[ignore].
Implement C++ boolean_fuse, boolean_cut, boolean_common using BRepAlgoAPI_Fuse/Cut/Common. Wire into OcctKernel::execute for Union/Difference/Intersection ops.
Write failing test for Fillet: create 20mm box, fillet with 2mm radius, verify resulting shape exports to valid STEP. Fill in boundary test fillet_edges and remove #[ignore].
Implement C++ fillet_all_edges(shape, radius) using BRepFilletAPI_MakeFillet iterating all edges via TopExp_Explorer. Wire into OcctKernel::execute for Fillet op.

Write failing test for Translate + centroid query: create 10mm box (centroid at origin), translate by (0.05, 0, 0), query Centroid, assert x ≈ 0.05. Fill in boundary test translate_centroid and remove #[ignore].

Implement C++ translate_shape(shape, dx, dy, dz) using BRepBuilderAPI_Transform with gp_Trsf::SetTranslation, rotate_shape(shape, ax, ay, az, angle) with gp_Trsf::SetRotation. Implement C++ query_centroid(shape) using BRepGProp::VolumeProperties + GProp_GProps.CentreOfMass(). Also implement query_area and query_bbox. Wire Translate/Rotate into execute(), Centroid/SurfaceArea/BoundingBox into query().

Write failing test for Tessellation: create sphere r=10mm, tessellate with tolerance 0.1, assert vertices.len() > 0, indices.len() % 3 == 0, normals present. Fill in boundary test tessellation_valid_mesh and remove #[ignore].

Implement C++ tessellate_shape(shape, tolerance) using BRepMesh_IncrementalMesh for meshing, then TopExp_Explorer(TopAbs_FACE) + BRep_Tool::Triangulation to extract vertices/indices/normals. Wire into OcctKernel::tessellate(). Implement Sphere primitive via C++ make_sphere(radius) using BRepPrimAPI_MakeSphere.

Write failing test for invalid reference error: create OcctKernel, execute Union with non-existent handle IDs, assert GeometryError::InvalidReference returned. Fill in boundary test invalid_reference_error and remove #[ignore].

Add handle validation in OcctKernel::execute before dispatching to C++: for ops referencing handles (Boolean, Fillet, Chamfer, Translate, Rotate), check shape store contains the referenced ID, return GeometryError::InvalidReference if not. Same validation in query/export/tessellate.

Write failing test for zero-dimension error: execute GeometryOp::Box with width=0.0 (or negative), assert GeometryError::OperationFailed returned. Fill in boundary test zero_dimension_error and remove #[ignore].

Add input validation in OcctKernel::execute before calling C++: for primitives, validate dimensions are positive (width, height, depth, radius > 0), return GeometryError::OperationFailed with descriptive message if not. For Fillet/Chamfer validate radius/distance > 0.

design_decisions.decision: Use cxx bridge for FFI instead of raw extern C or bindgen
design_decisions.rationale: cxx provides memory-safe C++ interop with UniquePtr for automatic cleanup of TopoDS_Shape objects. Avoids manual pointer management and use-after-free risks. The cxx bridge also generates compile-time-checked bindings.

design_decisions.decision: Thin C++ wrapper layer around OCCT APIs
design_decisions.rationale: OCCT has complex template-heavy C++ APIs that don't map cleanly to cxx bridge. A thin wrapper provides simple function signatures (make_box, boolean_fuse, etc.) that cxx can bind. Exception handling (try/catch Standard_Failure) is done in C++ and converted to Result strings.

design_decisions.decision: Box primitive centered at origin via offset
design_decisions.rationale: GeometryOp::Box spec says 'centered at origin' but BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox creates from corner. Wrapper calls MakeBox(gp_Pnt(-w/2, -h/2, -d/2), w, h, d) to center it. This matches expected centroid at (0,0,0) for the translate_centroid test.

design_decisions.decision: Fillet applies to ALL edges of the target shape
design_decisions.rationale: GeometryOp::Fillet has target + radius but no edge selector. Following the simplest M1 behavior: iterate all edges via TopExp_Explorer(TopAbs_EDGE) and add each to BRepFilletAPI_MakeFillet. Edge selection can be added in M2.

design_decisions.decision: Value::as_f64() extraction at Rust boundary, pure f64 across FFI
design_decisions.rationale: GeometryOp parameters are Value::Scalar with SI units. The Rust layer extracts f64 via as_f64() (which returns si_value in meters) before calling into C++. The C++ wrapper operates purely in meters. This keeps the FFI layer simple and type-safe.

design_decisions.decision: Query results as Value::Real for scalar quantities, panic-free for centroid/bbox
design_decisions.rationale: Volume/SurfaceArea return Value::Real(f64). Centroid could return a tuple but Value has no tuple variant — will return Value::Real for the primary coordinate or encode as Value::String JSON. Need to check what the boundary test expects. Most likely centroid test only checks a single displaced coordinate.

MockGeometryKernel uses next_id counter and records operations. OcctKernel uses same pattern: HashMap<u64, UniquePtr<OcctShape>> with monotonic next_id. Each execute() stores the resulting shape and returns GeometryHandle with ReprKind::Solid.

design_decisions.decision: OCCT linked as system libraries via pkg-config or OCCT_DIR env var
design_decisions.rationale: OCCT is a large C++ framework (~300MB compiled). Building from source in build.rs would be prohibitively slow. Instead, build.rs uses pkg-config to find installed OCCT, with OCCT_DIR environment variable as fallback for non-standard installations. CI environments need OCCT pre-installed.

reuse.how: Use to extract SI-unit f64 from GeometryOp parameter Values before passing to C++ FFI. Handles Int, Real, and Scalar variants uniformly.
Mirror the next_id counter + HashMap pattern for OcctKernel, replacing Vec<GeometryOpRecord> with HashMap<u64, UniquePtr<OcctShape>>.
Use in all test code to create Value::Scalar lengths in millimeters (converts to SI meters internally).
Reuse same GeometryHandle { id: GeometryHandleId(n), repr: ReprKind::Solid } pattern for returning handles from OcctKernel::execute().
Use GeometryError::InvalidReference(id), GeometryError::OperationFailed(msg), ExportError::*, TessError::*, QueryError::* directly — they are already well-defined.
reify-kernel-occt is already declared as a workspace dependency path. Just add it to reify-eval's [dev-dependencies] section.
